question_title: What will US airline passenger volume be for these weeks in April 2026, according to the TSA? (Apr 20–26, 2026)
summary:
Forecasting TSA passenger volume for April 20-26, 2026 (Mon-Sun). Three main estimation approaches were used:

1. **2024 comparable** (Apr 22-28, 2024 = 17.18M): The cleanest calendar/Easter-adjusted late-April Mon-Sun week, with ~3% two-year growth applied.
2. **2025 comparable** (Apr 7-13, 2025 = 17.41M): Normal non-Easter-adjacent April Mon-Sun week, with ~0.5% crisis-moderated growth.
3. **2026 seasonal ratio**: March 2026 normal weeks (~17.73M) adjusted by historical April/March seasonal ratio (~0.975).

Key context: The Iran war jet fuel crisis has nearly doubled fuel prices and trimmed airline capacity growth from +2.4% to +1.1%, but demand remains strong. Early April 2026 TSA data shows +1-2% YoY growth over 2025, indicating the crisis hasn't materially reduced passenger volumes. The target week is a normal late-April week (no holidays, 15 days post-Easter). Monte Carlo simulation with 100k draws incorporating crisis deepening risk (15% probability of significant further cuts) produces a median of ~17.44M with modest left skew.

factors.description: 2024 Apr 22-28 comparable (cleanest normal late-April Mon-Sun week): 17,178,886. With 2-year organic growth of ~3%, suggests ~17.35M center.
2025 Apr 7-13 comparable (normal non-Easter April Mon-Sun week): 17,405,722. Shows YoY growth trend and provides higher anchor.
Early April 2026 actual TSA data shows +1-2% YoY vs 2025, indicating modest growth persists despite fuel crisis already underway.
Jet fuel crisis: prices nearly doubled, Strait of Hormuz closure, United cutting ~5% routes in Q2-Q3, total capacity growth trimmed from +2.4% to +1.1%. Analysts warn crisis could deepen.
Demand remains strong per airline executives - better than 2025. Supply constraint not demand destruction. Passengers on cut routes likely rebook.
Late April tends to be ~2% lower than early April. 2024 showed Apr 22-28 was 2% below Apr 1-7.

reasoning:
**Forecast: TSA Passenger Volume, April 20-26, 2026**

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **Median (P50)** | 17,442,000 |
| **80% CI** | [16,932,000 - 17,861,000] |
| **50% CI** | [17,211,000 - 17,657,000] |

**Key reasoning:**
- Normal late-April Mon-Sun weeks in 2024-2025 ranged 17.18M-17.41M
- Early April 2026 TSA data shows +1-2% YoY growth despite the Iran war fuel crisis
- The crisis affects airline profitability/capacity, not passenger demand (executives say demand is "strong" and better than 2025)
- United's 5% route cuts and overall capacity trim from +2.4% to +1.1% reduce volumes modestly
- The distribution has slight left skew to account for the possibility the crisis deepens further before the target week

resolution_criteria:
This question is a subquestion of a group question. This subquestion specifically pertains to (and resolves according to) the option 'Apr 20–26, 2026'. The resolution criteria for the parent question is below. 

These subquestions resolve as the total volume of passengers screened by the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) at its checkpoints for the rolling 7-day periods in the listed options.

TSA posts the data at the following link:
[https://www.tsa.gov/travel/passenger-volumes](https://www.tsa.gov/travel/passenger-volumes)
fine_print: This question's information (resolution criteria, fine print, background info, etc) is synced with an [original identical question](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43112) which opened on 2026-04-06 23:04:00. This question will resolve based on the resolution criteria and fine print of the linked original question. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.
